title: The VALIS Trilogy — Page 209
raw_text:
dying girl wants to cook me a dinner, he thought. A dinner I don’t want to eat.
I’ve got to say no to her. I’ve got to keep her out of my dome. The insistence
of the weak, he thought; their dreadful power. It is so much easier to throw a
body block against the strong!
“Thank you,” he said. “I’d like it very much if we had dinner together. But
make sure you keep in radio contact with me on your way over here—so I’ll
know you’re okay. Promise?”
“Well, sure,” she said. “Otherwise—” She smiled. “They’d find me a
century from now, frozen with pots, pans and food, as well as synthetic
spices. You do have portable air, don’t you?”
“No, I really don’t,” he said.
And knew that his lie was palpable to her.
